title: Sikkim Becomes India's First Paperless Judiciary State
clat_relevance_summary: Directly relevant to CLAT Legal Reasoning. Sikkim's courts adopted e-filing, electronic summons, and digital case status displays. Tests knowledge of judicial digitization initiatives and the constitutional mandate for access to justice under Article 21.

title: Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026 Notified by MHA
clat_relevance_summary: High CLAT priority. MHA notified CAA Rules, 2026, specifying documents for citizenship applications and District Level Committee roles. Covers Constitutional Law (Art. 11, Art. 14), citizenship law, and the CAA controversy — a frequent topic in GK and Legal sections.

title: Right to Safe Road Travel is a Fundamental Right: Supreme Court
clat_relevance_summary: Landmark SC judgment expanding Article 21 (Right to Life) to include safe, motorable roads. Classic CLAT legal reasoning territory — fundamental rights, judicial expansion of Article 21, and PIL jurisprudence. Expect MCQs on the constitutional basis.

title: Justice Sanjiv Khanna Appointed as Chairperson of NALSA
clat_relevance_summary: NALSA (National Legal Services Authority) is established under the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987. CLAT tests knowledge of legal aid bodies, free legal services (Art. 39A), and senior SC judiciary appointments. Know the mandate: provide free legal aid to marginalised sections.

title: Ministry of Health Releases National Guidelines for Brain Death
clat_relevance_summary: Relevant to CLAT Legal GK on the Transplantation of Human Organs and Tissues Act, 1994. Brain death certification is the legal gateway for organ donation. Tests medico-legal ethics, consent law, and Art. 21 (right to die with dignity) jurisprudence.

title: SEBI Introduces 'Performance Validation Agency' for Mutual Funds
clat_relevance_summary: SEBI, constituted under the SEBI Act, 1992, introduced PVAs to verify mutual fund performance claims. Relevant to CLAT GK on financial regulators, investor protection law, and the regulatory framework for capital markets. Know SEBI's quasi-judicial powers.

title: RBI Eases Regulatory Norms for Small NBFCs
clat_relevance_summary: RBI's regulatory role over NBFCs flows from the RBI Act, 1934 and NBFC Master Directions. CLAT GK frequently tests RBI's mandate, NBFC classification, and MSME credit policy. Key: RBI is not directly under Parliament — it reports to the Finance Ministry.

title: REC Limited Signs MoU with PFC for Rs 1.12 Lakh Crore Co-financing
clat_relevance_summary: REC (Rural Electrification Corporation) and PFC (Power Finance Corporation) are Navratna PSUs under the Ministry of Power. Tests CLAT knowledge of PSU classifications, renewable energy law, and government financing mechanisms.

title: NPCI & Juspay Partner for 'Banking Connect' Platform
clat_relevance_summary: NPCI operates India's payment systems including UPI under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007. CLAT tests digital payments infrastructure, fintech regulation, and RBI's oversight role in payment systems.

title: IPPB Launches Zero-Balance SHG Savings Account
clat_relevance_summary: India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) serves financial inclusion under the Payments Bank framework. SHGs (Self-Help Groups) are linked to NABARD and women empowerment schemes. Relevant to CLAT GK on financial inclusion, women's rights, and cooperative banking law.

title: SIDBI Launches 'Swavalamban Connect Kendra' in 100 Districts
clat_relevance_summary: SIDBI (Small Industries Development Bank of India) is established under the SIDBI Act, 1989. Supports MSME ecosystem. CLAT tests knowledge of development finance institutions, MSME Act, 2006, and government's rural entrepreneurship programs.

title: MoSPI Releases 'Women and Men in India 2025' Report
clat_relevance_summary: MoSPI's statistical report shows improved sex ratio (910) and rising female labor participation. CLAT GK covers gender equality law, constitutional provisions (Art. 15, 39), and key welfare indices. Know the difference between sex ratio at birth vs. total sex ratio.

title: NITI Aayog Unveils 'DPI@2047' Roadmap
clat_relevance_summary: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015. Its DPI roadmap covers Aadhaar, UPI, and data frameworks for a $30T economy by 2047. CLAT tests the constitutional status of Aadhaar, data privacy (PDP Act), and federal planning mechanisms.

title: Cabinet Approves Creation of 5 New Districts in Delhi
clat_relevance_summary: Delhi's special constitutional status (Art. 239AA) allows the L-G and elected govt to manage administration. Creating new districts is an executive power. Tests CLAT knowledge of Delhi's unique federal position — NCT governance disputes have been major SC cases.

title: India-EFTA TEPA Ratified
clat_relevance_summary: EFTA comprises Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein. TEPA is India's first trade pact with EFTA, covering pharmaceuticals and investment. CLAT tests international trade law, WTO framework, and treaty-making powers under Art. 253 of the Constitution.

title: India and New Zealand Conclude FTA Negotiations
clat_relevance_summary: FTAs are trade agreements reducing tariffs. The India-NZ FTA covers dairy, services, and tech. CLAT GK: Parliament must ratify trade treaties under Art. 253. Know the difference between FTA, CEPA (Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement), and CECA.

title: India Ranks 4th in Global Military Spending 2025: SIPRI Report
clat_relevance_summary: SIPRI (Stockholm International Peace Research Institute) is a key source for CLAT GK on defence spending. India's Rs 85.4 billion spend reflects the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defence. Tests knowledge of defence procurement policy, indigenization targets, and SIPRI's role.

title: Jordan Joins ISA, CDRI, and Global Biofuels Alliance
clat_relevance_summary: All three — ISA (HQ: Gurugram), CDRI, and GBA — are India-led multilateral initiatives. CLAT tests international environmental law, climate agreements (Paris Accord), and India's soft power diplomacy. Know ISA's treaty status and its connection to the UN framework.

title: BRICS MENA Meeting 2026 Held in Riyadh
clat_relevance_summary: BRICS expanded in 2024 to include 5 new members. The MENA coordination highlights Global South trade in local currencies. CLAT GK tests BRICS membership, NDB (New Development Bank) functions, and India's foreign policy priorities.

title: UNESCO Adds 12 New Global Geoparks
clat_relevance_summary: UNESCO Global Geoparks protect geological heritage under international law. Relevant to CLAT GK on UNESCO conventions (World Heritage Convention, 1972), international environmental bodies, and India's geopark applications. Tests knowledge of UNESCO's mandate.

title: THE Asia University Rankings 2026: IISc Bangalore Ranks 32nd
clat_relevance_summary: IISc (Indian Institute of Science) is an autonomous institution of national importance. CLAT GK tests knowledge of higher education regulators — UGC Act, 1956, NAAC, NIRF rankings, and the difference between deemed, central, and state universities.

title: Odisha CM Launches PMGSY Phase-IV
clat_relevance_summary: PMGSY (Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme to connect rural habitations. CLAT tests Centre-State financial relations, Schedule VII (concurrent/state lists), and welfare schemes under DPSP (Art. 38, 39, 43).

title: Namo Stall Scheme Launched to Support Street Vendors
clat_relevance_summary: Linked to the PM SVANidhi scheme and Street Vendors (Protection of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Act, 2014. CLAT tests knowledge of livelihood rights (Art. 19(1)(g)), urban governance, and right to work under DPSPs.

title: MSJE Launches SMILE–Beggary Survey Mobile App
clat_relevance_summary: The SMILE scheme (Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise) rehabilitates beggars and transgender persons. CLAT tests Transgender Persons Act, 2019, rights of marginalized communities (Art. 21, 38), and social welfare legislation.

title: Mission Indradhanush 6.0 Launched for 100% Immunization
clat_relevance_summary: Universal Immunization Programme is a government health initiative linked to child rights under Art. 21A and the UNCRC. CLAT GK tests health law, the Epidemic Diseases Act, 1897, and the National Health Mission framework.

title: MoEFCC and NBA Launch 5-Year Biodiversity Governance Project
clat_relevance_summary: The Biological Diversity Act, 2002 establishes the NBA and BMCs. CLAT frequently tests environmental laws — Biological Diversity Act, Wild Life Protection Act, Forest Rights Act. BMCs protect community biological resources at the grassroots level.

title: India's 1st Hybrid Solar-Wind Power Plant Commissioned in Rajasthan
clat_relevance_summary: Renewable energy projects are governed by the Electricity Act, 2003 and the National Solar Mission. CLAT tests energy law, climate commitments (NDCs under Paris Agreement), and the constitutional basis for electricity regulation (Entry 38, Concurrent List).

title: NITI Aayog Report on Effective City Government
clat_relevance_summary: The 74th Constitutional Amendment (Nagarpalika Act) governs urban local bodies (ULBs). CLAT tests ULB powers (Schedule 12), direct election of Mayors, and the constitutional mandate for decentralization. SCs have ruled on states' failure to empower ULBs.

title: TN Overtakes Gujarat as Top Marine Fish Producer 2025: CMFRI
clat_relevance_summary: Marine fisheries are governed by the Marine Fishing Regulation Acts of respective states (a State subject). CLAT GK tests Centre-State jurisdiction in fisheries, EEZ (Exclusive Economic Zone) under UNCLOS, and coastal regulation zones (CRZ notifications).

title: Gita Gopinath Awarded 'Global Economist of the Year 2026'
clat_relevance_summary: Gita Gopinath is the First Deputy MD of IMF (HQ: Washington D.C.). CLAT GK tests the IMF/World Bank Bretton Woods framework, SDR (Special Drawing Rights), India's IMF quota, and prominent Indian-origin international personalities.

title: Google Cloud to Establish AI Hub in Visakhapatnam
clat_relevance_summary: AI governance is a emerging CLAT topic with the Digital India Act under development. Tests knowledge of India's data protection framework (DPDP Act, 2023), FDI policy in tech, and SEZ/IT corridor laws governing tech investments.

title: Adani Ports Signs 30-year Concession for Dar es Salaam Port, Tanzania
clat_relevance_summary: Port concession agreements are governed by international commercial law and bilateral investment treaties (BITs). CLAT tests India's maritime policy, the Sagarmala Project, and India-Africa diplomatic relations under IAFS (India-Africa Forum Summit).

title: MoES Secretary Inaugurates C-ART Weather Testbed at Visakhapatnam
clat_relevance_summary: The Ministry of Earth Sciences governs IMD, INCOIS, and NCESS. CLAT GK tests knowledge of disaster management law (DM Act, 2005), NDMA's role, and constitutional provisions for disaster relief (Art. 282, Concurrent List Entry 29).

title: Department of Posts Signs MoU with DTDC for Last-Mile Delivery
clat_relevance_summary: India Post operates under the Indian Post Office Act, 1898 (amended 2023). The MoU boosts e-commerce logistics. CLAT tests the updated Post Office Act, 2023 — which removed criminal penalties for postal employees — and DPSPs on livelihood.

title: PM Modi Launches Projects Worth Rs 20,000 Crore in Uttar Pradesh
clat_relevance_summary: Infrastructure projects like highway expansion are governed by the National Highways Act, 1956 and land acquisition under the RFCTLARR Act, 2013. CLAT tests land acquisition law, fair compensation, and constitutional provisions on property rights (Art. 300A).
